changes.before: Wet stone, slipping hooves, Cyndia riding ahead with one hand out to judge the width of the path. Then the coast opened below them: six berths of dock, a net yard gone pale with salt, houses crouched under the cliff, and beyond them the North Sea, flat and hard under a sky only slightly darker.
changes.after: Wet stone, slipping hooves, Cyndia riding ahead with one hand out to judge the width of the path. Then the coast opened below them: six berths of dock, a net yard gone pale with salt, houses crouched under the cliff, and beyond them the North Sea, flat and hard under a darker sky.

changes.before: The stumble showed how close she had come.
That landed harder than the gold.
Work took the next hour whole.
The knock of the stone against her ribs hit harder than the sight of the cairn.
The hours became that exchange repeated with small variations cruel enough to keep it from becoming routine.
Force would fail. Force always made the answer larger.
changes.after: Brute force would only bring the wind back harder.

changes.before: "The body keeps old lessons longer than the mind does."
changes.after: "I can know better and still keep pushing."

changes.before: Simple as an order. Difficult as anything she had done this year.
No softening. No lie about the distance.
Too late for comfort. Not yet too late for purpose.
changes.after: Too late for comfort, not too late to keep after Arthas.

changes.before: Too late for comfort. Not yet too late for purpose.
changes.after: Too late for comfort, not too late to keep after Arthas.
